mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in aggregate demand that suppresses corporate earnings growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with out-of-sample accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output that immediately reduces short-term equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle expansions, with Rank IC exceeding 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.5% while the unemployment rate exceeds 7%, accommodative monetary policy offsets weak labor data, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns as markets price in continued central bank support.
failure_condition: The joint condition of low rates and high unemployment yields negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5% annually signals insufficient economic expansion to support corporate profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 65% of quarters where GDPC1 annualized growth is below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate earnings and prompt expectations of tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases show no predictive power for negative 60-day returns, with Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 signal robust economic activity that boosts consumer confidence and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases > 200k fail to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, with directional accuracy below 52%.
